items.myth: Cross-validation prevents overfitting in the trained model.
items.reality: CV mitigates overfitting during selection and tuning by averaging across folds—it does not shrink model capacity. Regularization, simpler models, or early stopping still matter.
items.example: A deep unregularized net wins inner CV on 200 rows with high fold std—CV picked it, but it still memorizes noise without penalty or stopping.

items.myth: The best inner CV score is an honest estimate of test performance.
items.reality: Inner CV scores rank hyperparameters on shared partitions. After trying many settings, the best inner mean is optimistically biased—use nested outer scores or a locked test set.
items.example: GridSearchCV reports best_score_ 0.89 accuracy; nested outer mean 0.82 on the same dataset.

items.myth: More hyperparameter trials on the same CV folds always help mitigation.
items.reality: Larger search spaces increase tuning optimism on fixed folds. Nested CV, held-out test sets, or simpler search budgets counter overfitting the validation process.
items.example: Expanding RF grid from 12 to 200 configs raises inner best AUC 0.02 with no outer nested gain.

items.myth: CV for mitigation and CV for evaluation are unrelated workflows.
items.reality: They share the same fold machinery (see Model Evaluation → Cross-Validation). Mitigation emphasizes selection and nested tuning; evaluation emphasizes protocol definition and reporting.
items.example: StratifiedKFold syntax is identical; mitigation adds GridSearchCV and nested outer loops on top.
